description: Transform SalesBlock from passive execution tool into active habit-building cockpit. Applies Fogg (Tiny Habits), Clear (Atomic Habits), and Eyal (Hooked) behavioral design principles. 4 features + visual cue improvements for the notes system.

techStack.frontend: React 18 + TypeScript 5.3 + Vite 5.1 + TanStack Query v5 + Tailwind 3
techStack.existing: Day Planner, SalesBlocks, Goals, Analytics, Knowledge Graph Notes already built

designPrinciples: Goldilocks Zone: targets 4% above current capacity — stretch without breaking
Tiny Habits: lowest-friction entry point to break cold start inertia
Implementation Intentions: specific time + place commitments are 3x more effective than vague plans
Identity-based change: 'I am a top prospector' > 'I want to make 50 calls'
Visual feedback must be immediate, obvious, and celebratory
Every interaction should reduce friction to the next action

userStories.title: Visual typing cues for [[wikilink]] and #tag in NoteBlockEditor
userStories.description: As a rep typing notes, I need clear visual cues showing that [[ triggers entity linking and # triggers tagging, similar to how @ works in email or / shows commands in Slack.
acceptanceCriteria: Placeholder text reads: 'Type a note... Use [[ to link people & accounts, # for tags'
When user types [[ a subtle inline indicator appears (e.g., blue underline or highlight on the [[ characters)
Autocomplete dropdown has a header showing 'Link to...' with entity type icons
When user types # a tag-colored indicator appears on the # character
Tag autocomplete shows existing tags with color dots, plus 'Create new tag' option
Empty state in autocomplete shows 'Keep typing to search...' not blank
Keyboard hints shown: arrow keys to navigate, Enter to select, Esc to dismiss
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Currently [[ triggers autocomplete but there's no visual indicator that this is possible. New users won't discover the feature. Reference: Gmail @ mention UX, Notion / command UX, Obsidian [[ link UX.

userStories.title: Bring NoteBlockEditor to ContactDetailPage
userStories.description: As a rep viewing a contact, I need the graph-native NoteBlockEditor replacing the flat notes field so I can create linked notes with [[ references directly from the contact view.
acceptanceCriteria: ContactDetailPage 'Contact Notes' section uses NoteBlockEditor instead of flat textarea
NoteBlockEditor receives contactId prop — notes are scoped to this contact AND the contact's account
If contact has no account_id, notes are created with account_id = null (contact-only notes)
BacklinksPanel appears below notes showing what references this contact
[[ autocomplete works within contact notes, searching accounts, other contacts, deals
Existing flat notes data is preserved (read-only display above new editor if notes field has content)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Currently ContactDetailPage has a flat textarea for notes. Replace with NoteBlockEditor but handle the case where contact has no account_id (knowledge graph notes require account_id in the current schema — may need to make it nullable or create an 'unlinked' account).

userStories.title: Bring NoteBlockEditor to SalesBlock session cockpit
userStories.description: As a rep in a SalesBlock session working a contact, I need to see and add graph-native notes about the current contact/account without leaving the session.
acceptanceCriteria: SalesBlockSessionPage right panel includes a collapsible 'Notes' section
NoteBlockEditor pre-scoped to current contact's account_id and contact_id
Recent notes for this contact/account shown (last 5, expandable)
BacklinksPanel shows what references the current contact
Notes persist after session ends (they're in note_blocks table, not session-scoped)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This closes the loop: rep sees knowledge context DURING the session, not just when preparing.

userStories.title: Goldilocks Adaptive Goal System — weekly growth targets
userStories.description: As a rep starting my week, I need the system to propose stretch goals that are 4% above my recent average, with a safety valve for bad weeks.
acceptanceCriteria: New component: WeeklyGrowthGoalCard shown on Dashboard and Day Planner on Mondays
System calculates 7-day rolling average for calls, emails, meetings from activities table
Proposed target = rolling average * 1.04 (4% stretch), rounded to nearest whole number
If previous week attainment was < 60%, show 'Stability Mode' option (maintain current target)
User can accept, adjust, or dismiss the proposed target
Accepted target creates/updates a weekly goal in the goals table with metric and period
Progress bar shows current week progress vs accepted target throughout the week
New Supabase migration: add goal_source column to goals ('manual' | 'adaptive_suggested')
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Based on Goldilocks Zone research: peak performance happens at ~4% above current capacity. This replaces arbitrary goal-setting with data-driven stretch targets.

userStories.title: Micro-Momentum Warmup Session (Tiny Habits cold start breaker)
userStories.description: As a rep who hasn't started selling yet today, I need a low-friction 5-minute warmup session to break the cold start and build momentum.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard shows 'Warmup' button when no SalesBlock session has been active for 2+ hours
Warmup button also appears on Day Planner when first block hasn't started
Clicking 'Warmup' creates a 5-minute SalesBlock with session_type='warmup'
Warmup session has simplified task options: 'Social Touches', '3 Quick Emails', or 'LinkedIn Browse'
Timer is locked to 5 minutes (no extension during warmup)
On completion, debrief celebrates the small win with encouraging copy
Debrief includes prominent 'Launch Full Block' button to convert momentum into a real session
'Launch Full Block' pre-fills a 25-minute or 90-minute session based on user's typical pattern
New session_type value: 'warmup' added to SalesBlock types
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fogg's Tiny Habits: make the behavior so small it's impossible to fail. 5 minutes of social touches is the 'two push-ups' of sales. The transition from warmup to full block captures momentum at its peak.

userStories.title: Implementation Intention Scheduler (Golden Hour commitment)
userStories.description: As a rep ending my day, I need to commit to a specific time block for tomorrow's first prospecting session so it becomes an appointment, not a choice.
acceptanceCriteria: Commitment Prompt appears during Daily Debrief or when last SalesBlock of the day completes
Also appears on Friday afternoon debrief for Monday planning
User selects a 60-minute window from their available time tomorrow (respects calendar events)
App creates a Google/Outlook calendar invite with: title 'SalesBlock: Prospecting', deep link to pre-configured session
Calendar invite uses existing OAuth connection (Google Calendar or Outlook Calendar)
If no calendar connected, shows 'Connect calendar in Settings' prompt
Committed time shows as a 'locked' block on next day's Day Planner timeline
Reminder notification at T-15 minutes (if notification permissions granted)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implementation Intentions research: 'I will [behavior] at [time] in [location]' makes follow-through 3x more likely. The calendar invite makes the commitment external and visible.

userStories.title: Identity-Based Feedback Loops in session debrief
userStories.description: As a rep completing a session, I need the debrief to reinforce my professional identity rather than just showing raw numbers.
acceptanceCriteria: During onboarding (or Settings), user selects a Sales Persona from 4 options
Personas: 'The Relentless Closer', 'The Relationship Builder', 'The Strategic Hunter', 'The Precision Sniper'
Each persona has: name, icon, color, and identity-reinforcement copy templates
Post-session debrief replaces '25 Calls Logged' with persona-specific copy
Example: 'Identity Confirmed: You acted like a Relentless Closer today. 25 bricks added to your pipeline.'
Streak tracking: consecutive days of meeting persona-aligned targets shown as 'Identity Streak: 5 days'
New Supabase migration: add sales_persona column to users table
Settings page includes persona selector with descriptions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: James Clear (Atomic Habits): 'Every action is a vote for the type of person you wish to become.' The debrief should make the rep feel like they ARE the persona, not just chasing numbers.

userStories.title: Social Proof sidebar — Peers in the Zone
userStories.description: As a rep working a session, I need to see that teammates are also actively working right now to create social accountability.
acceptanceCriteria: Small sidebar element on SalesBlockSessionPage shows 'Peers in the Zone'
Shows count and first names of teammates currently in active SalesBlock sessions
Data from salesblocks table where status='in_progress' and org_id matches
Updates every 60 seconds via polling (not real-time — avoid WebSocket complexity)
If no peers active, shows encouraging fallback: 'You're leading the charge today'
Privacy: only shows first names, not activity details
Can be collapsed/hidden via user preference
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Social proof is one of the strongest behavioral triggers. Knowing someone else is working right now makes it harder to quit. Keep it lightweight — polling, not WebSockets.
